question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 misconfigures its eBGP neighbor toward R1 with `remote-as 65099`
instead of `65001`. R1's locally-configured AS (65001) no longer matches
what R2 announces in OPEN. The BGP session never reaches Established;
R1 emits a NOTIFICATION (Bad Peer AS) and the eBGP adjacency flaps idle.
All eBGP-learned prefixes from R1 (192.88.99.0/32 .. .10/32) drop out
of the AS 65000 RR mesh.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), the eBGP neighbor toward R1 is configured with remote-as 65099 instead of R1's real AS 65001.
The AS that R2 expects no longer matches the AS R1 presents, so BGP's peer-AS check fails and the eBGP session cannot be established between R2 and R1.
Consequence: with no established session, R1's eBGP-learned prefixes (192.88.99.0/32 ..) are not exchanged and drop out of the AS 65000 route-reflector mesh. Fix: set the R1 neighbor's remote-as back to 65001 on R2.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R1: neighbor 192.0.0.6.
- R2: neighbor 192.0.0.5.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R1 (1 instance):
- R1: Changed state to NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
no neighbor 192.0.0.5
!
neighbor 192.0.0.5
 remote-as 65099
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy PASS in
  route-policy PASS out

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
no neighbor 192.0.0.5
!
neighbor 192.0.0.5
 remote-as 65001
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy PASS in
  route-policy PASS out

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:42:26.583 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.0.6 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:42:26.580 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.0.5 Down - Remote AS configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 65099)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:42:26.588 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to NSR-Ready
